nicknames: The Godfather of Los Angeles Basketball
player.archetype: fearless point guard, cultural connector, creator-investor

system.title: The World Around the Game
system.core_thesis: Baron Davis is the proof point for a player world that moves from neighborhood imagination to national basketball myth to technology-backed storytelling.
system.audience_feeling: A young fan should feel the movement from South LA possibility, to Westside opportunity, to UCLA pressure, to NBA swagger, to the We Believe roar, to the current creator-investor chapter.
system.world_structure: A compact spherical memory world with seven zones that can be crossed in minutes, built like a playable documentary with missions, outfits, NPCs, story objects, and player interview unlocks.

main_quest.name: The Route
main_quest.description: The user carries memory objects across Baron's eras. Each delivery unlocks a story prompt that Baron can later answer in his own voice or use to interview another player.

interaction_rules: One story object delivery per zone
social_rules: No open text chat in version one
3D emoji wheel for follow me, thanks, fire, respect, wait, hoop, story, and laugh
Users can guide each other through movement and jumping
rights_light_rules: Use fictional team and school marks until official licenses are approved
Use inspired colors and silhouettes instead of exact jerseys in the prototype
Use original audio beds until music rights are cleared
Mark exact childhood block details as player-confirmation pending
Treat all first-person memories as placeholders until Baron records or approves them

evidence.claim: Messenger uses a tiny sphere, dynamic camera, central gravity, seven distinctive areas, hand-modeled detail, randomized clothing and hair, and small multiplayer rooms capped around 10 players.
evidence.use: Use seven Baron zones, compact spherical navigation, one mission per zone, smooth outfit switching, and calm social presence.

evidence.claim: Messenger pairs a calm and strange feeling with a small planet layout, outlines, color choices, hand-drawn graphics, and optimized 3D detail.
evidence.use: Make Baron world stylized, warm, personal, and optimized rather than photoreal.

evidence.claim: Messenger is a WebGL and Three.js browser game where players can change clothes and hair and send 3D emoji.
evidence.use: Make the prototype browser-first with clothes, hair, and emoji as reusable player-world controls.

evidence.claim: UCLA lists Davis as Los Angeles, Crossroads School, and UCLA, and notes his Gatorade, McDonald's, Parade All-American, Crossroads state title, Pac-10 Freshman of the Year, and ACL injury after a dunk against Michigan.
evidence.use: Ground the Crossroads and UCLA zones, with a VHS and recovery mission.

evidence.claim: NBA lists Davis as a 6 foot 3 guard from UCLA, born April 13, 1979, drafted in 1999 round 1 pick 3, with career averages of 16.1 points, 3.8 rebounds, and 7.2 assists.
evidence.use: Ground the draft, NBA identity, and career arc.

evidence.claim: NBA.com says Davis brought steady swagger, was drafted No. 3 by Charlotte, led the No. 8 seeded We Believe Warriors over the No. 1 seeded Mavericks in 2007, averaged 25.0 points in that series, and returned to Los Angeles with the Clippers in 2008.
evidence.use: Make We Believe the emotional sports peak and Clippers the hometown return.

evidence.source_name: Los Angeles Sentinel SoCal Legends profile
evidence.claim: The article calls Davis the Godfather of Los Angeles Basketball, places his childhood in South Central Los Angeles near Manchester Blvd and San Pedro with his grandparents, and traces him through Crossroads, UCLA, Golden State, Clippers, Drew League, and Black Santa Company.
evidence.use: Anchor the South LA opening and the Los Angeles identity, while keeping exact block details confirmation pending.

evidence.claim: Baron's site describes him as a two-time NBA All-Star, serial entrepreneur, investor, and creator of content and digital platforms at the intersection of sports, technology, business, and culture.
evidence.use: Make the final chapter a creator-investor studio rather than only an epilogue.

evidence.claim: BRIC describes Davis as founder of No Label, SLiC, B.I.G., UWish, and More Than Us, and as an investor, funder, producer, and advocate for financial literacy, ed-tech, and VC leaders from underrepresented communities.
evidence.use: Use Baron as the formatter's host, interviewer, investor, and trusted story connector.

evidence.source_name: U.S. Chamber Business of Sports interview
evidence.claim: The U.S. Chamber article says Davis moved from a 13-year professional basketball career into entrepreneurship, built Business Inside the Game to connect athletes and business ecosystems, founded Sports, Lifestyle in Culture, invested in companies including Vitaminwater, WNBA, Kitu Super Coffee, Sleeper, Thrive, Cann Drinks, Tracklib, Toucan, and Vidy, and sees AI, NIL, and creator-owned distribution as major sports and entertainment forces.
evidence.use: Make the final chapter a business ecosystem where athlete-owned stories, sponsorship, creator distribution, NIL, investors, advisors, and emerging technologies connect.

evidence.claim: CLF connects cultural leaders to technology companies, advances more African Americans into technology, and coinvests across consumer, crypto, enterprise, fintech, games, and bio plus health.
evidence.use: Frame the player-world system as sports, culture, technology, and Black wealth generation infrastructure.

evidence.source_name: A16Z CLF LinkedIn post with Baron Davis
evidence.claim: The post and transcript say Baron is excited about AI for information and how creators, writers, directors, and social media people can translate information into video, animation, and storytelling.
evidence.use: Make the final zone an AI story engine that turns player interview information into playable worlds.

zones.title: 85th & Avalon: First Court
zones.era_role: Childhood, family, first hoop imagination, and South LA origin point
zones.fan_feeling: The user should feel small inside a big basketball dream, with the city acting like both pressure and propulsion.

mission.name: Take the Shoes to the Court
mission.description: Pick up a worn pair of hoop shoes near the home block and carry them to the chain-link court. When delivered, the court lights up with kid voices and ball bounce.

zones.story_unlock_prompt: Who first made you believe your game could take you beyond the block, and what did that belief cost them?

zones.title: Westside Jump: Crossroads
zones.era_role: The bridge from South LA roots to Santa Monica opportunity
visuals: Bus route ribbon from South LA to the coast
Trophy case with fictionalized championship icons
zeitgeist: 1990s backpack, pager, and gym bag details
zones.fan_feeling: The user should feel the tension of being from one world while learning to dominate another.

mission.name: Deliver the Permission Slip
mission.description: Carry a school pass from the bus stop to the Crossroads gym before warmups. NPCs open dialogue about belonging, talent, and the first time local hype becomes national.

zones.story_unlock_prompt: What did Crossroads teach you about moving between worlds without losing where you came from?

zones.title: Westwood: UCLA Rise
zones.era_role: College pressure, Los Angeles family proximity, national spotlight, and injury recovery
zeitgeist: Family and friends close enough to attend games
zones.fan_feeling: The user should feel the rush of a local star becoming national, then the vulnerability of the body interrupting destiny.

mission.name: Fix the Tape
mission.description: Find three VHS fragments across campus: freshman flash, the Michigan dunk, and the rehab room. Assemble them in the dorm to unlock the story card.

zones.story_unlock_prompt: When the injury happened, what did you learn about who you were when explosiveness was temporarily taken away?

zones.title: Teal Prologue: Hornets
zones.era_role: First NBA identity, draft pressure, road life, and learning the business of the league
visuals: Teal and purple inspired city block
zones.fan_feeling: The user should feel the speed of entering the league before fully knowing what the league expects from you.

mission.name: Find the Missing Play
mission.description: Carry a scribbled play card from the rookie locker to the arena floor. NPCs teach invisible rules about veterans, film, travel, and confidence.

zones.story_unlock_prompt: What did the NBA teach you in your first year that no coach, scout, or magazine could have prepared you for?

zones.title: Oracle Roar: We Believe Warriors
zones.era_role: Signature myth, fan electricity, underdog identity, and one of the loudest Baron memories
visuals: Crowd sound waves visible in the air
zones.fan_feeling: The user should feel a city deciding to believe before the rest of basketball caught up.

mission.name: Find the Roar
mission.description: The checklist only says find the sound. Follow the rising crowd rumble, deliver a rally towel to the tunnel, and watch the arena open.

zones.story_unlock_prompt: What did Oracle sound like from inside your body before the rest of the country understood that series was real?

zones.title: Staples Homecoming: Clippers and LA
zones.era_role: Return to Los Angeles as a pro star and hometown mirror
zeitgeist: Kids seeing a local player come home
zones.fan_feeling: The user should feel the complicated pride of returning home, where love and expectation arrive together.

mission.name: Sign the Home Court Board
mission.description: Collect signatures from kids, coaches, and fans across the plaza, then carry the board into the tunnel before tipoff.

zones.story_unlock_prompt: What changed when you came home as Baron Davis the NBA player instead of Baron Davis the LA kid?

zones.title: Future Court: A16Z Story Studio
zones.era_role: Baron as investor, creator, interviewer, and bridge between sports culture and technology
zeitgeist: Athletes as founders and media owners
NIL turning athlete attention into owned enterprise
Players connecting sponsors, investors, advisors, and creators
zones.fan_feeling: The user should feel that player memory can become a new kind of media company, with Baron as the person players trust to open the door.

mission.name: Matchmake the Story Engine
mission.description: Collect ideas from a young hooper, a filmmaker, a retired player, a sponsor, an investor, and a founder. Connect them on the BIG board, then deliver the finished story seed to unlock the next player-world portal.

zones.story_unlock_prompt: What kind of business can only happen when athletes own the archive, the interview, the fan space, and the technology layer?

interview_questions: What place from this era still feels alive when you close your eyes?
What object would instantly take you back to this chapter?
What did young fans misunderstand about you then?
What did teammates know about that era that fans never saw?
What was the private pressure behind the public highlight?
What sound, smell, outfit, or phrase belongs in this world?
Which story would you only tell another player?
Who else should have a world after yours?
data_gaps: Confirm 85th Street and Avalon Boulevard directly with Baron before presenting it as factual geography.
Confirm whether South LA should be represented by exact streets, emotional composites, or fictionalized landmarks.
Confirm which NBA, Warriors, Hornets, Clippers, UCLA, Crossroads, and Staples Center references can be licensed.
Confirm whether Baron wants his childhood, family, and grandmother story depicted directly or symbolically.
Confirm actual player voice, archival photos, highlights, and music rights for the licensed build.
Confirm which NBA friends Baron wants as the first three plug-and-play formatter tests.
